lede: A look at August 31 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jang Won-young and Andrew Foster (footballer), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2025 Tarasin landslide
events.desc: A landslide in the Darfur region of Sudan kills over 1000 people. 26

events.title: Typhoon Rusa
events.desc: Typhoon Rusa, the most powerful typhoon to hit South Korea in 43 years, made landfall, killing at least 236 people.

events.title: Diana, Princess of Wales
events.desc: Diana, Princess of Wales, her partner, Dodi Fayed, and driver Henri Paul die in a car crash in Paris.

events.title: Kyrgyzstan
events.desc: Kyrgyzstan declares its independence from the Soviet Union.

events.title: Letter bomb
events.desc: A parcel bomb sent by Ngô Đình Nhu, younger brother and chief adviser of South Vietnamese President Ngô Đình Diệm, fails to kill King Norodom Sihanouk of Cambodia.

events.title: Democratic Army of Greece
events.desc: The retreat of the Democratic Army of Greece into Albania after its defeat on Gramos mountain marks the end of the Greek Civil War.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Serbian paramilitary forces defeat Germans in the Battle of Loznica.

events.title: Mary Ann Nichols
events.desc: Mary Ann Nichols, the first of Jack the Ripper's confirmed victims, is murdered.

events.title: 1886 Charleston earthquake
events.desc: The 7.0 Mw Charleston earthquake strikes southeastern South Carolina with a maximum Mercalli intensity of X (Extreme), killing 60 people and causing damage worth an estimated at $5 to $6 million.

events.title: Al-Kamil
events.desc: Al-Kamil becomes sultan of the Ayyubid dynasty.

deaths.text: Thomas Bavin, New Zealand-Australian politician, 24th Premier of New South Wales

observances.text: Baloch-Pakhtun Unity Day (Balochs and Pashtuns, International observance)
